title: Build-context overlay in v1.44 makes using buildah in container harder
body:
### Issue Description

We use buildah containerized (in a Kubernetes Pod, via Tekton) with native overlay. Up to and including v1.43.1, this only required mounting a host directory over `/var/lib/containers/storage` or the equivalent rootless path (assuming the host directory is on a filesystem that supports overlay).

Version 1.44 adds an overlay over the build context directory and puts the `upper` and `work` directories to `/var/tmp` by default, which creates another obstacle for containerized usage (perhaps unnecessarily?).

Workarounds:
1. Mount a host dir over `/var/tmp` as well
2. Set `engine.image_copy_tmp_dir = "/var/lib/containers/storage"` (or equivalent rootless path) in `containers.conf`

Looks like a good workaround but doesn't work:
1. Set `engine.image_copy_tmp_dir = "storage"` in `containers.conf`
    - this makes buildah attempt to put the overlay under `<containers storage path>/tmp`, but fails with `Error: mounting an overlay over build context directory: creating temporary directory: stat /var/lib/containers/storage/tmp: no such file or directory`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a Containerfile for installing buildah + dependencies:

    ```Dockerfile
    FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi10/go-toolset:1.26.3 AS go-build

    ARG BUILDAH_TAG=v1.44.0

    USER 0

    # Install dependencies for compiling buildah
    RUN dnf -y install \
            bzip2 \
            glib2-devel \
            gpgme-devel \
            libassuan-devel \
            libseccomp-devel

    WORKDIR /build

    RUN git clone --branch=$BUILDAH_TAG https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah && \
        cd buildah && \
        make bin/buildah


    FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi10/ubi-minimal:10.1

    USER 0

    RUN microdnf -y install \
            containers-common-extra \
            gpgme \
            libassuan \
            libseccomp

    COPY --from=go-build /build/buildah/bin/buildah /usr/local/bin/buildah

    COPY <<EOF /etc/containers/storage.conf
    [storage]
    driver = "overlay"
    EOF

    ENV BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
    ```

2. Build it: `buildah build -t buildah:1.44.0 .`
3. Try to run a build:

    ```bash
    podman run --rm -i -v /var/lib/containers/storage buildah:1.44.0 bash <<EOF
    mkdir /tmp/buildcontext
    cd /tmp/buildcontext
    echo 'FROM scratch' > Containerfile
    buildah build
    EOF
    ```

    ```text
    Error: mounting an overlay over build context directory: creating overlay scaffolding for build context directory: mount overlay:/var/tmp/buildah-context-855737399/overlay/890669428/merge, data: lowerdir=/tmp/buildcontext,upperdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-855737399/overlay/890669428/upper,workdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-855737399/overlay/890669428/work,userxattr: invalid argument
    ```

4. Try again with `image_copy_tmp_dir = "storage"`:

    ```bash
    podman run --rm -i -v /var/lib/containers/storage buildah:1.44.0 bash <<EOF
    mkdir /tmp/buildcontext
    cd /tmp/buildcontext
    echo 'FROM scratch' > Containerfile

    mkdir /etc/containers/containers.conf.d
    printf '[engine]\nimage_copy_tmp_dir = "storage"' >/etc/containers/containers.conf.d/set-tmp-dir.conf

    buildah build
    EOF
    ```

    ```text
    Error: mounting an overlay over build context directory: creating temporary directory: stat /var/lib/containers/storage/tmp: no such file or directory
    ```

### Describe the results you received

Containerized usage now requires a non-obvious volume mount or configuration change

### Describe the results you expected

Ideally, expected the build context overlay to be created in the container storage dir by default and just work.
Alternatively, expected the `engine.image_copy_tmp_dir = "storage"` setting to solve the problem.

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.44.0
Go Version:      go1.26.3 (Red Hat 1.26.3-4.el10_2)
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.3.0
image Version:   5.40.0
Git Commit:      30a4189415e5d0a3a0c5971307a6432d8ed1a097
Built:           Wed Jun  3 13:55:30 2026
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
{
    "host": {
        "Distribution": {
            "distribution": "\"rhel\"",
            "version": "10.1"
        },
        "MemFree": 3450511360,
        "MemTotal": 33055125504,
        "OCIRuntime": "crun",
        "SwapFree": 8589930496,
        "SwapTotal": 8589930496,
        "arch": "amd64",
        "cpus": 14,
        "hostname": "123b86d8a1a5",
        "kernel": "7.0.9-102.fc43.x86_64",
        "os": "linux",
        "rootless": true,
        "uptime": "7h 40m 7.94s (Approximately 0.29 days)",
        "variant": ""
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",
        "GraphImageStore": "",
        "GraphOptions": null,
        "GraphRoot": "/var/lib/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {
            "Backing Filesystem": "btrfs",
            "Native Overlay Diff": "true",
            "Supports d_type": "true",
            "Supports shifting": "false",
            "Supports volatile": "true",
            "Using metacopy": "false"
        },
        "GraphTransientStore": false,
        "ImageStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "RunRoot": "/run/containers/storage"
    }
}
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
We're hitting the same problem with `STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs` and `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` in a GitLab CI runner (Kubernetes executor).

Our setup uses a custom image based on Wolfi with buildah, where we build OCI images inside the CI job. With v1.44, the unconditional overlay mount over the build context fails even though we explicitly use the `vfs` storage driver:

```
Error: mounting an overlay over build context directory: creating overlay scaffolding for build context directory: mount overlay:/var/tmp/buildah-context-852256633/overlay/377000136/merge, data: lowerdir=/builds/...,upperdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-852256633/overlay/377000136/upper,workdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-852256633/overlay/377000136/work: invalid argument
```

The build context overlay appears to bypass the storage driver setting entirely - setting `STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs`, passing `--storage-driver vfs` on the CLI, or pointing `CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF` to a custom config with `driver = "vfs"` all have no effect. The overlay mount is attempted regardless.

We had to pin buildah to 1.43.1 as a workaround. It would be great if the build context overlay either respected the configured storage driver or had a fallback/opt-out when the overlay mount fails.

Another issue I found with this overlay mount is that it is not marked as volatile (does not have [volatile](https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/container-volatile-overlay-mounts) mount option). Thus, unmount of this overlay on a busy CI host is taking really long time.

I was able to workaround the problem; however, I don't like it too much.

```
  before_script:
    - mkdir -p "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.buildah-tmp"
    - export TMPDIR="$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.buildah-tmp"
```
same error here with gitlab kubernetes executor. Workaround helps but not optimal
